_metadata.title: Operation Epic Fury — Day-by-Day OSINT Timeline
_metadata.note: Auto-generated backfill. Values marked null are unconfirmed. Verify against primary sources.

sources: militarytimes.com - Six dead, 18 service members injured in Iran operation
jpost.com - Two more US servicemembers killed in Iran
military.com - Three US service members killed, several injured Operation Epic Fury
cibercuba.com - US reveals names of four soldiers killed

YCharts - WTI Crude Oil Spot Price (Historical Data)
Barchart - Crude Oil Prices Soar as Strait of Hormuz Closed to Tanker Traffic

key_operations: Task Force Scorpion Strike (one-way attack drones)

casualties.notes: Initial assault casualties not separately reported for Day 1. By March 1-2, cumulative US KIA reached 3-6 and WIA reached 5-18. Iranian Red Crescent reported 555 total Iranian deaths by mid-day EST on February 28 (initial assault).
casualties.confidence_explanation: Day 1 (Feb 28) casualty figures are not explicitly broken out in search results. Available data conflates initial assault casualties with subsequent days' totals. Only Iranian civilian estimate (555) is directly attributed to the initial assault timeframe.

notes.iran_ships_destroyed: 9 naval craft destroyed within hours of initial attack on Feb 28, including Iranian drone carrier Shahid Bagheri. CENTCOM later claimed all 11 ships in Gulf of Oman destroyed, but verification incomplete as of reporting date.
notes.us_ships_damaged: No US naval vessel damage reported in first 24 hours. Iranian ballistic missile and Shahed drone struck near US 5th Fleet NSA facility in Bahrain (200m from HMS Juffair) but no direct US ship hits documented.
notes.hormuz_status: Strait remained open Feb 28 with maritime warnings issued. Iran's IRGC called for closure but no successful blockade implemented on Day 1.
notes.mines_detected: No mine detection data reported in available sources for Feb 28, 2026.

notes.brent_oil_usd: Interpolated from Feb 27 (66.96) and Feb 26 (65.10) WTI prices; Brent typically trades 6-7 USD/bbl above WTI[5]
notes.wti_oil_usd: Feb 27, 2026 closing price of 66.96 USD/bbl used as proxy for Feb 28[5]
notes.hormuz_flow_pct: Not reported in search results; Strait of Hormuz closure confirmed as of March 3, 2026[1]
notes.shipping_disruption: Confirmed: Barchart headline states 'Crude Oil Prices Soar as Strait of Hormuz Closed to Tanker Traffic' dated March 3, 2026[1]
notes.confidence_rationale: WTI price data is reliable but Feb 28 specific data unavailable (using Feb 27 proxy). Hormuz closure timing and shipping disruption percentage unconfirmed in provided sources.

notes.iran_missiles_fired_context: Iran launched at least 162 missiles at Israel on March 1, with a sharp decline from 58 missiles in late evening Feb 28 to 29 in late evening to 12 by March 1 afternoon
notes.intercept_data_limitation: Search results confirm CENTCOM intercepted 'hundreds of Iranian missile and drone attacks' but do not provide specific numbers for March 1 alone
notes.damage_limitation: No specific damage figures from Iranian missile strikes reported in search results for March 1

economic.notes: Search results do not contain specific data for March 1, 2026. The earliest available WTI data in results is March 2, 2026 (71.13 USD/barrel). No data found for Brent on March 1, 2026. Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic and shipping disruption metrics are not present in provided search results. Operation Epic Fury context cannot be verified from commodity price data alone.

casualties.notes: US KIA confirmed at 6 by Secretary Hegseth and General Caine briefing on March 4, 2026[5]. US WIA (seriously wounded) reported as 5 from initial Iranian attacks on March 1-2[1][2]. Iran military and civilian casualty figures not provided in available sources as of March 4.

naval.hormuz_status: IRGC claimed complete control, but no Iranian ships underway per US statements; effectively closed due to risks

missiles.damage_from_missiles: Damage to Al-Udeid Air Base in Qatar; extent undisclosed. No casualties reported.

air.notes: Only one-way attack drone shots down 73% from opening days is confirmed from available search results. Specific aircraft destroyed counts, IADS sites neutralized, and US aircraft losses are not reported in the provided search results as of March 5, 2026. Search results contain data through March 4, 2026 briefings but lack granular March 5 specific metrics.

proxy.notes: Search results contain information through March 5, 2026, but do not provide specific quantified data for Hezbollah rocket launches, Houthi attacks, Iraqi militia attacks, or proxy casualty figures for March 5 specifically. Available data shows: March 2 (several projectiles), March 3 (approximately 30 rockets per Ynet), March 4 (drone and missile launches). No Houthi or Iraqi militia attack data found in search results.
proxy.confidence_explanation: Search results lack specific operational data for March 5, 2026. Only Hezbollah-Israel front is documented; Houthi and Iraqi militia activity absent from provided sources.

cyber_diplomatic.note: The provided search results contain no specific information about cyber incidents, diplomatic developments, UN actions, or ceasefire talks on March 6, 2026. Search results focus on casualty figures, military operations, and strategic objectives as of late March 2026, but do not include granular daily reporting for March 6.

sources: armyrecognition.com - US Surpasses 6,000 Combat Sorties in Operation Epic Fury

key_operations: US surpassed 6,000 combat sorties by March 7
Intensified strikes on Iran's missile, drone, and naval forces
military_ops.notes: Search results lack specific Day 8 (March 7) metrics. Only sorties figure is available from a source referencing March 7 operations. Specific strike counts and target numbers for this date are not reported in provided search results. Later updates show 8,000+ targets struck by Day 22 (March 21) and 10,000+ by Week 4, but granular Day 8 data is unavailable.

air.notes: Specific metrics for March 7, 2026 are not available in search results. Available data points to earlier dates: By March 5, over 1,000 targets struck in first 72 hours; by March 25, over 10,000 targets struck total with two-thirds of Iran's missile/drone/naval production facilities destroyed. Admiral Cooper reported on March 5 that ballistic missile attacks decreased 90% and drone attacks decreased 83% since day one.
air.reason_for_null_values: Search results do not contain granular daily breakdowns for March 7, 2026 specifically. Data is aggregated across multi-day periods or provided for other dates within the operation.

proxy.notes: On March 7, 2026, Hezbollah conducted extensive rocket and missile attacks on northern Israel. The IDF reported that a Hezbollah projectile near the Lebanese border caused injuries to 8 Israeli soldiers, with 5 sustaining severe injuries, including the son of Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich. Israel responded with airstrikes on Hezbollah headquarters in Beirut's southern suburbs and military sites in southern and eastern Lebanon. Houthi and Iraqi militia activity for this specific date is not reported in available sources.

cyber_diplomatic.note: The provided search results contain no specific information about cyber incidents, diplomatic developments, UN actions, or ceasefire talks on March 8, 2026. The search results focus on military casualty figures (as of March 24-25), operation objectives, and general campaign updates from early March, but do not include detailed daily reporting for March 8 specifically.
